question: [File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / withdrawCollateral (bitmap consistency invariant fuzz)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer perform any sequence of supplyCollateral and withdrawCollateral calls on victim's position, under PRECONDITIONS where victim has authorized EcrecoverAuthorizer and attacker has been granted authorization, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [arbitrary sequence of supplyCollateral and withdrawCollateral calls], violating INVARIANT that collateral bitmap bit i must be set iff collateral[i] > 0, causing scoped impact: any inconsistency between bitmap and collateral balances causes health checks to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral? Proof idea: stateful fuzz test asserting that after every supplyCollateral and withdrawCollateral call, for all i in [0, 127]: (collateralBitmap >> i) & 1 == 1 iff collateral[i] > 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / liquidate (bitmap consistency after liquidation fuzz)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER trigger any sequence of liquidations on victim's position, under PRECONDITIONS where victim has multiple active collaterals and is liquidatable, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [arbitrary sequence of liquidate calls with varying seizedAssets and repaidUnits], violating INVARIANT that collateral bitmap bit i must be set iff collateral[i] > 0, causing scoped impact: any inconsistency between bitmap and collateral balances after liquidation causes subsequent health checks to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral? Proof idea: stateful fuzz test asserting that after every liquidate call, for all i in [0, 127]: (collateralBitmap >> i) & 1 == 1 iff collateral[i] > 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral (bitmap bit set when oldCollateral > 0)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer call supplyCollateral(market, i, assets, victim) where collateral[i] > 0 (bit i already set), under PRECONDITIONS where victim already has active collateral at index i, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; submit; supplyCollateral(i, assets, victim)], violating INVARIANT that collateral bitmap must match actual deposited collateral, causing scoped impact: the condition oldCollateral == 0 && assets > 0 is false (oldCollateral > 0), so bit i is NOT re-set (already set), collateral[i] increases — is there any overflow or bitmap corruption from supplying to an already-active index? Proof idea: fuzz assets and assert that supplyCollateral to an already-active index never modifies collateralBitmap and correctly increases collateral[i].

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / take (seller debt increase with bitmap)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer call Midnight.take on behalf of victim (as seller) to increase victim's debt, under PRECONDITIONS where victim has authorized EcrecoverAuthorizer and attacker has been granted authorization, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; submit; take(offer, ..., victim, ...) where victim is seller], violating INVARIANT that every borrow/take must create correct debt and collateral obligation, causing scoped impact: attacker increases victim's debt without victim's consent, potentially making victim's position unhealthy and liquidatable, with the bitmap unchanged but the debt/collateral ratio now unfavorable? Proof idea: assert that after attacker-initiated take with victim as seller, victim's debt increases and isHealthy is checked correctly against the existing bitmap.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / repay (bitmap unchanged after full repayment)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer call Midnight.repay(market, debt, victim, ...) to fully repay victim's debt, under PRECONDITIONS where victim has debt and multiple active collaterals, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; submit; repay(market, victim.debt, victim, ...)], violating INVARIANT that collateral bitmap must match actual deposited collateral, causing scoped impact: after full repayment, victim's debt = 0 but collateralBitmap still has all bits set — isHealthy returns true (debt=0 skips oracle loop) but the bitmap is not cleared, leaving ghost bits that could cause DoS if oracles later revert? Proof idea: assert that after full repayment, collateralBitmap is unchanged and isHealthy returns true without calling any oracle.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral (MidnightBundles as authorized operator)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER use MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget to supply collateral at an unintended index for victim, under PRECONDITIONS where victim has authorized MidnightBundles (which checks isAuthorized(taker, msg.sender)), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [victim authorizes MidnightBundles; attacker calls supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with collateralSupplies targeting an unintended index], violating INVARIANT that collateral bitmap must match actual deposited collateral, causing scoped impact: MidnightBundles activates a new bitmap bit for victim at an unintended index, potentially DoSing health checks if the oracle for that index reverts? Proof idea: assert that MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget correctly validates that collateralIndex is intended by the taker.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / isHealthy (bitmap loop with clearBit correctness)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER trigger a state where the isHealthy bitmap loop's clearBit operation produces an incorrect result due to the bit index being >= 128, under PRECONDITIONS where collateralBitmap has a bit set at index >= 128 (impossible for uint128 but worth testing), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [attempt to set bit >= 128 via any path], violating INVARIANT that collateral bitmap must correctly represent active collaterals, causing scoped impact: if any path allows setting a bit >= 128 in the uint128 bitmap, the msb() function using clz opcode could return an index >= 128, causing market.collateralParams[i] to revert with array out of bounds? Proof idea: assert that setBit and clearBit in UtilsLib always operate on indices in [0, 127] and that no code path passes an index >= 128.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / liquidate (RCF check uses collateral[collateralIndex] not bitmap)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer manipulate victim's collateral[collateralIndex] to be 0 while bit collateralIndex is still set in the bitmap, under PRECONDITIONS where victim is unhealthy and collateral[collateralIndex] = 0 but bit is set, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; manipulate to create ghost bit; liquidate(collateralIndex, 0, repaidUnits, victim)], violating INVARIANT that repay amount, seize amount, LIF, close factor, and bad-debt realization must be mutually consistent, causing scoped impact: the RCF check uses _position.collateral[collateralIndex] which is 0, so maxRepaid = 0 * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * WAD / lif = 0, and any repaidUnits > 0 would violate the RCF check? Proof idea: assert that if collateral[collateralIndex] = 0 and bit is set, liquidate with repaidUnits > 0 reverts with RecoveryCloseFactorConditionsViolated.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / isHealthy (multi-collateral health check skips index due to bitmap)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer withdraw all collateral from index i (clearing bit i) while victim has debt backed by both index i and index j, under PRECONDITIONS where collateral at j alone is sufficient to back the full debt (so isHealthy passes after withdrawal), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; submit; withdrawCollateral(i, collateral[i], victim, attacker)], violating INVARIANT that collateral cannot be withdrawn if it would make debt unhealthy, causing scoped impact: attacker steals victim's collateral at index i, the bitmap correctly clears bit i, but victim's health is now backed only by index j — if oracle[j] later drops, victim is liquidatable with less collateral than before? Proof idea: assert that after authorized withdrawal of collateral[i], isHealthy only considers collateral[j] and that the health check is correct.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral (bitmap bit set for index with adversarial oracle returning type(uint256).max)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer supply 1 wei at index j where oracle[j].price() returns type(uint256).max, under PRECONDITIONS where victim has debt and oracle[j] returns type(uint256).max, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; submit; supplyCollateral(j, 1, victim)], violating INVARIANT that multi-collateral valuation must not overflow, causing scoped impact: isHealthy computes maxDebt += 1 * type(uint256).max / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD which may overflow uint256, causing isHealthy to revert and DoSing victim's withdrawCollateral and liquidation? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price near type(uint256).max and assert that isHealthy either correctly handles the overflow or reverts with a clear error.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / liquidate (bitmap loop badDebt computation with multiple active collaterals)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer activate N collateral indices for victim with varying amounts, then trigger a liquidation where the badDebt computation iterates all N bits, under PRECONDITIONS where victim is unhealthy with N active collaterals, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; supplyCollateral for N indices; wait for unhealthy; liquidate], violating INVARIANT that bad debt must reduce lender credit exactly once and proportionally, causing scoped impact: the badDebt computation uses zeroFloorSub iteratively — does the order of iteration (msb to lsb) affect the final badDebt value due to zeroFloorSub's non-commutativity? Proof idea: fuzz N collateral amounts and prices and assert that badDebt is independent of the order in which collaterals are iterated.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / withdrawCollateral (bitmap bit cleared when assets = 0)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer call withdrawCollateral(market, i, 0, victim, attacker) where collateral[i] > 0, under PRECONDITIONS where bit i is set and collateral[i] > 0, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; submit; withdrawCollateral(i, 0, victim, attacker)], violating INVARIANT that bitmap bit i must be set iff collateral[i] > 0, causing scoped impact: the condition newCollateral == 0 && assets > 0 is false (assets == 0), so bit i is NOT cleared, collateral[i] is unchanged — this is a no-op, but does it create any inconsistency? Proof idea: assert that withdrawCollateral with assets=0 never modifies collateralBitmap and never modifies collateral[i].

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral (bitmap bit set, then collateral reduced to 0 by liquidation without bit clear)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER trigger a liquidation with seizedAssets = collateral[i] - 1 (leaving 1 wei) followed by another liquidation with seizedAssets = 1 (clearing the bit), under PRECONDITIONS where victim is liquidatable and collateral[i] >= 2, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [liquidate(i, collateral[i]-1, 0, victim); liquidate(i, 1, 0, victim)], violating INVARIANT that bitmap bit i must be cleared when collateral[i] reaches 0, causing scoped impact: after the second liquidation, newCollateral = 0 and seizedAssets = 1 > 0, so bit i IS cleared — is there any intermediate state where bit i is set but collateral[i] = 0? Proof idea: assert that after each liquidation step, (collateralBitmap >> i) & 1 == 1 iff collateral[i] > 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / isHealthy (bitmap includes index not in market.collateralParams)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer set a bitmap bit at index i where i < market.collateralParams.length but the market was created with fewer collateral params than 128, under PRECONDITIONS where market has K < 128 collateral params and attacker activates index K-1, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; supplyCollateral(K-1, 1, victim)], violating INVARIANT that collateral bitmap must correctly represent active collaterals, causing scoped impact: isHealthy accesses market.collateralParams[K-1] which is valid, but what if the attacker activates index K (out of bounds)? Proof idea: assert that supplyCollateral with collateralIndex >= market.collateralParams.length always reverts before modifying the bitmap.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / liquidate (bitmap loop terminates correctly when bitmap = 0)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER trigger a state where victim's collateralBitmap = 0 but victim still has debt, under PRECONDITIONS where all collateral has been seized (all bits cleared) but debt remains, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [liquidate all collateral; victim still has debt], violating INVARIANT that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: the liquidate bitmap loop (while _collateralBitmap != 0) terminates immediately with maxDebt = 0 and badDebt = originalDebt, so the entire debt is realized as bad debt — is this the correct behavior and does it leave the bitmap in a consistent state? Proof idea: assert that after all collateral is seized (bitmap = 0), a subsequent liquidate with 0/0 correctly realizes all remaining debt as bad debt.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / isHealthy (bitmap bit set for index with LLTV = 0 — impossible but test)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer activate a collateral index with LLTV = 0 (not in allowed tiers, so market creation would revert), under PRECONDITIONS where market creation enforces isLltvAllowed, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [attempt to create market with LLTV = 0; touchMarket reverts], violating INVARIANT that market creation params must be valid, causing scoped impact: this path is blocked by isLltvAllowed check — but does the bitmap loop correctly handle a hypothetical LLTV = 0 entry (maxDebt contribution = 0)? Proof idea: assert that isLltvAllowed(0) returns false and that no market can be created with LLTV = 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / isHealthy (bitmap with all 128 bits set — impossible due to MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER = 16)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer attempt to set more than 16 bits in victim's bitmap, under PRECONDITIONS where victim has 16 active collaterals, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; supplyCollateral at 17th index], violating INVARIANT that TooManyActivatedCollaterals must be enforced, causing scoped impact: the 17th supplyCollateral reverts with TooManyActivatedCollaterals — but does countBits correctly count all 16 bits in a uint128 with non-contiguous set bits? Proof idea: fuzz bitmap patterns with exactly 16 set bits and assert countBits returns 16 for all such patterns.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / liquidate (bitmap bit set at index not matching liquidated collateral)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer activate index j for victim, then call liquidate(market, j, seizedAssets, 0, victim) where j is in the bitmap but the liquidation is intended for index i, under PRECONDITIONS where victim is liquidatable and has collateral at both i and j, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; supplyCollateral(j, 1, victim); liquidate(j, 1, 0, victim)], violating INVARIANT that liquidation must not leave accounting desynced, causing scoped impact: the liquidation seizes 1 wei from index j (clearing bit j if collateral[j] = 1), while the attacker intended to liquidate index i — does this create a bitmap state where index j is cleared but index i is still set with full collateral? Proof idea: assert that after liquidating index j, collateral[j] decreases by seizedAssets and bit j is cleared iff collateral[j] = 0, while collateral[i] and bit i are unchanged.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / isHealthy (differential test: bitmap-based vs direct collateral sum)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer create a state where the bitmap-based health check (iterating set bits) gives a different result than a direct sum over all 128 collateral slots, under PRECONDITIONS where bitmap and collateral array are inconsistent, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [any sequence of authorized operations that creates bitmap/collateral inconsistency], violating INVARIANT that collateral bitmap bit i must be set iff collateral[i] > 0, causing scoped impact: the bitmap-based health check skips active collateral (bit not set, collateral > 0) or includes inactive collateral (bit set, collateral = 0), leading to incorrect health determination? Proof idea: differential fuzz test comparing isHealthy result against a reference implementation that sums all 128 collateral slots directly.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / liquidate (bitmap bit set for index with price = type(uint128).max + 1)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer activate index j where oracle[j].price() returns a value such that collateral[j] * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE > type(uint128).max, under PRECONDITIONS where victim has debt and oracle[j] returns a very large price, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; supplyCollateral(j, largeAmount, victim)], violating INVARIANT that multi-collateral valuation must not overflow, causing scoped impact: the liquidate and isHealthy loops compute maxDebt += collateral[j] * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD — if this overflows uint256, the health check reverts, DoSing liquidation? Proof idea: fuzz collateral[j] and oracle price near overflow boundary and assert that the protocol correctly handles or rejects values that would cause overflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / isHealthy (bitmap bit set for index with price = 0 and collateral > 0)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer activate index j where oracle[j].price() returns 0 and supply a large amount of collateral, under PRECONDITIONS where victim has debt and oracle[j] returns 0, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; supplyCollateral(j, largeAmount, victim)], violating INVARIANT that multi-collateral valuation must not skip or double count collateral, causing scoped impact: isHealthy computes maxDebt += largeAmount * 0 / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD = 0, so the large collateral at index j contributes nothing to health — victim appears less healthy than they are, potentially allowing liquidation of a position that should be healthy if oracle[j] were non-zero? Proof idea: assert that isHealthy correctly returns false when oracle[j] = 0 and all other collateral is insufficient, and that this is the intended behavior.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / liquidate (bitmap bit set for index with maxLif = WAD — LLTV = WAD case)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer activate index j with LLTV = WAD (maxLif = WAD) and supply collateral, under PRECONDITIONS where victim has debt and market has a LLTV_8 = WAD collateral, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; supplyCollateral(j, amount, victim)], violating INVARIANT that repay amount, seize amount, LIF, close factor, and bad-debt realization must be mutually consistent, causing scoped impact: the badDebt computation uses mulDivUp(WAD, WAD, maxLif) = mulDivUp(WAD, WAD, WAD) = 1 for the maxLif term — does the LLTV=WAD case in the bitmap loop correctly compute badDebt without division by zero or incorrect rounding? Proof idea: fuzz collateral amount and price for LLTV=WAD index and assert badDebt computation is correct.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / isHealthy (bitmap bit set for index with collateral = type(uint128).max)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer supply type(uint128).max collateral at index j for victim, under PRECONDITIONS where victim has authorized EcrecoverAuthorizer and attacker has been granted authorization, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; submit; supplyCollateral(j, type(uint128).max, victim)], violating INVARIANT that multi-collateral valuation must not overflow, causing scoped impact: isHealthy computes maxDebt += type(uint128).max * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD — for price = ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE and lltv = WAD, this is type(uint128).max which fits in uint256 — but for larger prices, does this overflow? Proof idea: fuzz collateral amount near type(uint128).max and oracle price and assert that maxDebt computation never overflows uint256.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / liquidate (bitmap bit set for index not matching collateralIndex in liquidate)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer activate index j for victim (setting bit j), then call liquidate(market, i, seizedAssets, 0, victim) where i != j and i is also in the bitmap, under PRECONDITIONS where victim is liquidatable and has collateral at both i and j, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; supplyCollateral(j, amount, victim); liquidate(i, collateral[i], 0, victim)], violating INVARIANT that liquidation must not leave accounting desynced between borrower debt, lender credit, protocol balances, and seized collateral, causing scoped impact: after liquidating index i (clearing bit i), bit j remains set with collateral[j] > 0 — does the subsequent isHealthy correctly include collateral[j] in the health check? Proof idea: assert that after liquidating index i, isHealthy correctly iterates only the remaining set bits and includes collateral[j] in maxDebt.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: setIsAuthorized / supplyCollateral / isHealthy (bitmap bit set for index with collateral = 1 wei and high LLTV)] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer supply exactly 1 wei of collateral at index j with LLTV = 0.98e18 (LLTV_7), under PRECONDITIONS where victim has debt near the health boundary, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE [sign Authorization; supplyCollateral(j, 1, victim)], violating INVARIANT that healthy accounts must not become liquidatable due to rounding, causing scoped impact: the 1-wei collateral at index j contributes maxDebt += 1 * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * 0.98e18 / WAD which rounds down to 0 for most prices — does this dust collateral entry affect the health check result in a way that could make a healthy position appear unhealthy? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price and assert that 1-wei collateral contribution to maxDebt is correctly computed as 0 or 1 depending on price.
